steps.title: Express angle T in radians
steps.content:
Since angle T is 3 times angle S, and angle S = $\frac{8\pi}{13}$ radians:
$$T = 3 \cdot \frac{8\pi}{13}$$

steps.title: Convert from radians to degrees
steps.content:
Multiply by the conversion factor $\frac{180}{\pi}$:
$$T = 3 \cdot \frac{8\pi}{13} \cdot \frac{180}{\pi}$$
The $\pi$ cancels:
$$T = 3 \cdot \frac{8}{13} \cdot 180 = \frac{8}{13}(180)(3)$$
This expression matches choice B — B is correct.
